# pickled · llms.txt

Test what agents actually understand about your product.

Pickled is an open-source CLI that runs scenarios across a matrix of interfaces (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Anthropic API), sources (READMEs, docs URLs, llms.txt, codebase globs), and toolsets (`none` / `web` / `mcp`), then scores each cell with deterministic checks: expected facts, declared traps, citation grounding in controlled cells, and tool-use provenance in tool-enabled cells. No LLM grades another LLM.

## Install

```bash
bun add -g @pickled-dev/cli
```

## Commands

- `pickled init [path]` - scaffold a pickled.yml config in your repo.
- `pickled audit [path]` - static scan of agent-context files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, llms.txt). No LLM calls.
- `pickled check [path]` - run agent scenarios against registered sources.

## Minimum config

```yaml
tool:
  name: your-product
  description: One line about your product.

docs:
  sources:
    readme: ./README.md

scenarios:
  - name: Installation
    prompt: How do I install your product?
    requiredSources: [readme]

threshold: 80
```

## Verdict contract

Pickled has two orthogonal verdicts. Renderers must not conflate them.

- **Scenario verdict** answers whether one scenario satisfied the source and trap contract. Values: `YES`, `PARTIAL`, `NO`, `Error`. Rendered as `Well grounded`, `Grounded`, `Partially grounded`, `Trap fired`, `Ungrounded`, or `Error`.
- **Run verdict** answers whether the aggregate score met the configured threshold. Values: `run passes`, `run fails`. Renders only when a threshold is configured. Without a threshold, show `Overall: X / 100` and stop.

The scenario verdict determines the label family. Confidence may refine `YES` into `Well grounded` (>= 90) or `Grounded` (< 90), but it never upgrades `PARTIAL`, `NO`, `Trap fired`, or `Error`. The categorical signal wins.

## Source contract

Pickled does not magically introspect your product. You declare what counts as truth under `docs.sources` in `pickled.yml`. Anything not registered does not count for scoring. Anything an agent could read can be registered: README, llms.txt, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, JSDoc, inline comments, internal handbooks, hosted source bundles. Public or private. URLs or local paths. Loader types: `file` (default), `url`, `codebase` (glob expanded into one logical source; useful for registering whole directories of JSDoc or per-package agent docs).

## Matrix and toolsets

A scenario can expand into one cell per `(interface × source × toolset)` tuple. Each cell scores independently. Three toolset shapes ship today:

- `none`: deterministic baseline. The cell's active source content is injected into the agent's prompt. The citation contract applies if `requiredSources` is declared.
- `web`: WebSearch + WebFetch on Claude Code. Source is NOT injected; the cell's prompt names the active source as the discovery target. Tool-use provenance is required.
- `mcp`: any MCP server (`stdio`, `http`, `sse`) on Claude Code. Source is NOT injected; the agent invokes `mcp__<server>__*` tools at inference. Tool-use provenance is required.

Citation contract only applies in `none` cells. Non-`none` cells score on `expected.includes`/`excludes` plus tool-use provenance: a cell that satisfies `expected` but never invokes any of the configured tools is hard-vetoed to `NO` with confidence `0`, because an answer from model prior knowledge cannot testify to the tool path the cell is meant to test.

## Targets today

- `claude-code` - Claude Agent SDK
- `codex-cli` - Codex CLI binary
- `anthropic` - Anthropic API, direct SDK. Requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. No tools, no workspace; comparable to CLI targets but not identical.

Stubbed and not yet implemented: `gemini-cli`, `amazon-q`, OpenAI and Google API targets. Do not list these as supported in present tense.

## JSON contract

JSON output stays raw. It carries machine fields (`answerable`, `confidence`, `traps`, `citations`). Human labels like `Well grounded` are derived in each renderer, not present in the JSON.

## What to preserve when describing pickled

- It tests what agents understand. It is not a generic LLM eval framework.
- Scoring is deterministic by contract. No LLM grades the answer.
- "Stay fresh" is footer chrome only. The retired draft branding for the scoring metric is gone; do not reintroduce it.
- The pickle metaphor is a wink, not a theme. One body-copy mention per page, max.
- No em dashes in copy. Hyphens, colons, periods, or parentheses are fine.

- Agent context for working in this repo: https://github.com/caiopizzol/pickled/blob/main/AGENTS.md
